prs.title: test(router): use capturing group in simple capturing group test
prs.body: This PR fixes issue #1837 by correcting a test case in the common router tests. The 'Simple capturing group' test was incorrectly using a non-capturing group `(?:bar|baz)`, making it identical to the 'Non-capturing group' test. By changing it to `(bar|baz)`, we ensure that capturing groups are actually being tested as intended.

prs.title: Fix/issue 1837
prs.body:
Fix #1837: Correct "Simple capturing group" test case

This PR updates the failing "Simple capturing group" test in common.case.test.ts to align with expected router behavior.
The issue was caused by incorrect test expectations rather than implementation logic.
Tests were executed using the proper Vitest configuration to resolve missing globals.
All test cases now pass successfully after the fix.
This ensures more accurate test coverage and prevents false failures in the suite.

prs.title: feat(create-turbo): replace pnpm commands in README with selected pac…
prs.body:
Title: Simplify README commands based on selected package manager (Fixes #6585)

Description:

This PR improves the developer experience by customizing the generated README.md in create-turbo projects.

What’s changed

The README previously included command blocks for multiple package managers (npx, yarn, pnpm, etc.) across sections like build, dev, and link. This PR updates the generator to include only the relevant commands based on the package manager selected during project initialization.

prs.title: Fix transpose constructor check
prs.body:
Closes #4161

## Description

This PR fixes an inconsistency in the `transpose` function where the returned date is incorrectly shifted by the timezone offset, contrary to the documented behavior.

## Problem

The `transpose` function is expected to transfer date/time components between date classes (e.g., `Date` → `UTCDate`) without applying timezone shifts. However, due to a strict `isConstructor` check, the function sometimes falls back to `constructFrom`, which can invoke the constructor incorrectly and result in a standard `Date` object with shifted values.

## Changes

- Improved the `isConstructor` check to correctly identify valid date constructors  
- Ensured `transpose` consistently uses the intended constructor with `new`  
- Prevented fallback behavior that caused timezone-based shifts  
- Added a reproduction test case to validate correct behavior  

## Result

The `transpose` function now correctly preserves date/time components across different date classes without unintended timezone adjustments, aligning implementation with documentation.

prs.title: fix(types): lowercase CommonResponseHeadersList to match runtime beha…
prs.body:
## Description

This PR fixes a mismatch between runtime behavior and TypeScript definitions for response headers in axios.  
Since axios lowercases all header names at runtime, the existing Title-Case types caused incorrect typings.  
As a result, lowercase access worked at runtime but lost type safety, while Title-Case was typed but returned `undefined`.  
This update aligns the types with actual runtime behavior by lowercasing all entries in `CommonResponseHeadersList`.  
This ensures consistent, predictable, and type-safe access to response headers.

prs.title: docs: add Bun as a package manager option
prs.body:
Added [Bun](https://bun.sh/) as a package manager option in the README's Download section, alongside the existing Bower, npm, and Yarn options.
Bun is a modern, high-performance JavaScript runtime and package manager that is growing rapidly in adoption. Since milligram is published on npm, it works seamlessly with `bun add milligram`.
## Changes
- Added an "Install with Bun" section to `readme.md` after the Yarn section, following the same formatting pattern as the existing options.
This is a documentation-only change with no code impact.

prs.title: docs: add bun installation step to contributing guidelines
prs.body:
## Summary
The build script `scripts/build/package.sh` uses `bun` directly (for the CDN build step), but `CONTRIBUTING.md` never mentions installing it. This causes confusion for first-time contributors who follow the guide and get an error when running the build.
## Changes
- **Getting Started**: Added a new step to install [bun](https://bun.sh/) via `npm install -g bun` as a prerequisite, before forking/cloning.
- **Test build**: Added a note explaining that `bun` is required for the CDN build step, with a cross-reference back to the Getting Started section.
This is a documentation-only change with no code impact.

prs.title: feat: add agent skills for dotenv and dotenvx
prs.body:
## Description

This PR adds a new `skills/` directory with `SKILL.md` files for `dotenv` and `dotenvx` to provide structured guidance for handling environment variables and secrets.  
These files are designed to help AI coding agents and developers follow best practices when configuring projects.  
It includes standard usage for `dotenv` and advanced workflows like encryption and multi-environment support for `dotenvx`.  
A README has also been added to explain the purpose of the directory.  
This improves consistency, security, and developer experience when working with env configurations.

prs.title: Fix #24382: Zero-value columns render visible lines when borderWidth > 0
prs.body:
Closes #24382

## Summary
Fixes a regression introduced in v11.4.2 where zero-value columns render a visible horizontal line when `borderWidth > 0`.

## Problem
Zero-value points result in SVG `rect` elements with `height: 0`. Despite having no height, the stroke is still rendered, causing unwanted horizontal lines at the baseline.

## Expected Behavior
Zero-height columns should remain visually hidden, consistent with behavior prior to v11.4.2.

## Solution
Updated `pointAttribs` in `ts/Series/Column/ColumnSeries.ts` to conditionally disable the stroke when:
- point value is `0`
- computed column height is `0`

This ensures no border is rendered for zero-height columns.

## Notes
- Does not affect cases where `minPointLength` is set, since those columns have non-zero height and should render borders as expected.
- Change is limited in scope and does not impact non-zero values.

## Impact
Restores expected rendering behavior without affecting existing features.

prs.title: docs: Add missing --force flag documentation
prs.body:
## Description

This PR updates the Turbo CLI documentation to include the missing `--force` (`-f`) flag for the `turbo login` command.

## Problem

The `--force` flag is supported by the CLI but was not documented in the `login.mdx` reference page. This could lead to confusion for users who want to re-authenticate or overwrite existing credentials.

## Changes

- Added documentation for the `--force` (`-f`) flag in:
  - `apps/docs/content/docs/reference/login.mdx`
- Included a brief description explaining its purpose
- Added usage context to improve clarity for users

## Result

The documentation now accurately reflects the available CLI options, improving developer experience and consistency between the CLI and docs.

## Related Issue

Closes #10625

prs.title: fix(sql): allow not() to accept SQL | undefined (and/or return type)
prs.body:
## What
Fixes #1818

The [not()](cci:1://file:///Users/vaibhavsingh/Desktop/SESD_Workshop_2/yarn/drizzle-orm/drizzle-orm/src/sql/expressions/conditions.ts:165:0-176:65) function previously only accepted `SQLWrapper`, causing a TypeScript
error when composing it with [and()](cci:1://file:///Users/vaibhavsingh/Desktop/SESD_Workshop_2/yarn/drizzle-orm/drizzle-orm/src/sql/expressions/conditions.ts:104:0-124:1) or [or()](cci:1://file:///Users/vaibhavsingh/Desktop/SESD_Workshop_2/yarn/drizzle-orm/drizzle-orm/src/sql/expressions/conditions.ts:126:0-142:79), which both return `SQL | undefined`:

```ts
// ❌ Before — TypeScript error
not(and(ilike(users.name, '%asdf%')))

// ✅ After — works perfectly
not(and(ilike(users.name, '%asdf%')))

prs.title: fix: Use script-shell=bash for cross-platform with-shell-commands example
prs.body:
## Summary

- Reverts the inline `node -e` script replacements
- Adds `.npmrc` with `script-shell=bash` to ensure all shell commands (`mkdir -p`, `cat`, `echo >`) work on Windows via Git Bash

This is the same pattern Turborepo's own test suite uses (`crates/turborepo/tests/common/setup.rs`). It provides comprehensive cross-platform coverage without sacrificing readability or introducing fragile inline Node.js in a teaching example.

Fixes #11205.

prs.title: Implement TTY detection for Hono's logger middleware
prs.body:
Currently, the logger middleware always applies ANSI color codes regardless of whether
the output destination is a terminal. This causes garbled color escape sequences in
logs when output is piped to files, CI systems, or other non-TTY destinations.

This PR adds cross-runtime TTY detection to getColorEnabled() in src/utils/color.ts:
- Node.js / Bun: checks process.stdout.isTTY
- Deno: checks Deno.stdout.isTerminal() (with legacy isatty() fallback)
- Serverless / Workers: defaults to true (unchanged behavior)

The NO_COLOR env variable continues to take highest precedence.

prs.title: Feat/cartography chart 7124
prs.body:
Closes #7124

This PR adds native cartographic chart support to Recharts using `d3-geo`.

It introduces a new `<CartographyChart>` container along with supporting components like `<GeoPath>` and `<ChoroplethSeries>` for rendering GeoJSON data. The implementation follows existing Recharts patterns and integrates with the current charting infrastructure.

Projections can be configured using built-in options (e.g., `mercator`, `naturalEarth1`) or custom projection instances, with automatic scaling support via `fitExtent`.

Basic test coverage has been added to validate rendering and behavior, and all components are exposed through the public API.

Overall, this enables rendering map-based visualizations directly within Recharts while staying consistent with its existing API.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Map/cartography charting: new CartographyChart with GeoPath and ChoroplethSeries for rendering maps and choropleths
  * Multiple geographic projections supported and color-scaled choropleth rendering
  * Interactive region events (click, hover)

* **Documentation**
  * API docs updated to include new cartography components

* **Tests**
  * Test coverage added for cartography components and projection behavior

* **Chores**
  * D3 geographic integration and TypeScript typings added
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: fix(recharts): implement granular tag-aware SVG property filtering (#…
prs.body:
Fixes #3113

This PR makes the SVG prop filtering utilities aware of the specific SVG tag being rendered.

Previously, the filtering logic treated all SVG elements the same, which caused element-specific attributes (like `r` for `<circle>` or `d` for `<path>`) to leak into other elements. This could trigger React "unknown prop" warnings and result in invalid DOM attributes.

With this change, filtering is now tag-aware, ensuring that only valid attributes are applied to each SVG element. Existing components have been updated to pass their respective tag names, and test coverage has been extended to verify correct behavior.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Improvements**
  * More precise, tag-aware SVG attribute and event handling per element type (svg, groups, paths, circles, rectangles, etc.), reducing unintended attributes on SVG elements and improving rendering consistency.

* **Tests**
  * Added and expanded tests to verify tag-specific SVG prop filtering and event-handling behavior across SVG element types and various input scenarios.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: fix: add feedback when yarn publish runs with unchanged version
prs.body:
Fixes #2940

This PR adds a feedback message when `yarn publish` proceeds without a version change.

Previously, the command would continue silently, which could make it unclear whether a new version was actually published.

With this change, users now get a clear message indicating that the version remains unchanged, improving transparency during the publish process.

prs.title: Fix/gh 12574 clone custom classes
prs.body:
## Fix: Correct cloning of custom class instances (#12574)

### Problem

`clone()` was returning the original object for custom class instances due to default `valueOf()` behavior.

### Solution

* Use `valueOf()` only if it returns a different value (`val !== obj`)
* Otherwise fallback to proper cloning via `cloneObject()`
* Added explicit handling for functions to return as-is

### Verification

* Added test for custom class instances
* All existing tests pass
* Confirmed `clone(new MyClass()) !== originalInstance`

### Files Changed

* `lib/helpers/clone.js`
* `test/helpers/clone.test.js`

prs.title: Fix/gh 9232 default validation
Fix/cli no models msg
prs.body:
Description

This PR improves the developer experience when running prisma generate on a schema with no models defined.

Changes
Introduced a new --allow-no-models flag for the prisma generate command
Updated the error message when no models are present but required
Made the message clearer, more concise, and actionable by suggesting the new flag
Problem

Previously, running prisma generate on a schema without models resulted in a confusing or unhelpful error message, leaving users unsure how to proceed.

Solution
Added an explicit --allow-no-models flag to support valid use cases where no models are defined
Improved the error messaging to clearly guide users toward the correct action
Impact
Better developer experience and clearer guidance
Supports edge cases where schemas intentionally have no models
Reduces confusion for new users
Checklist
 Feature / UX improvement
 Backward compatible
 No breaking changes

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added `--allow-no-models` CLI flag to support running `prisma generate` without any models defined.

* **Documentation**
  * Updated guidance to recommend using `--allow-no-models` for projects using raw queries only.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: fix(adapter-mariadb): fix prepared statement leak
prs.body:
Fixes: #29364

Description

This PR fixes a prepared statement leak in @prisma/adapter-mariadb that could cause the database to hit the max_prepared_stmt_count limit over time.

Problem

In version 7.5.0, the adapter switched to using the binary protocol via client.execute(). The MariaDB Node.js driver internally caches prepared statements, but these statements are not always reliably closed on the server when evicted from the cache.

Since Prisma frequently generates dynamic SQL queries (for example, queries with varying IN clause sizes), this resulted in a large number of unique prepared statements accumulating on the server, eventually leading to resource exhaustion.

Solution

This PR reverts query execution back to the text protocol using client.query() inside MariaDbQueryable.performIO.

Avoids creating server-side prepared statements
Prevents unbounded growth of prepared statements
Aligns behavior with existing transaction handling in the adapter
Impact
Fixes server-side prepared statement leaks
Prevents hitting max_prepared_stmt_count
Improves stability for applications with dynamic query patterns
Checklist
 Bug fix (non-breaking change)
 Tested with MariaDB/MySQL
 No regression in existing functionality

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Prevents accumulation of server-side prepared statements in the MariaDB adapter, reducing connection/server resource leaks.
  * Improves reliability of transaction operations (BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK/SAVEPOINT).
  * Preserves large-number precision during queries to avoid lossy numeric conversions.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: fix: handle nested paths in optimisticConcurrency #16054
prs.body:
### Fix: optimisticConcurrency handling for nested paths (Closes #16054)

#### Description

Fixes incorrect `optimisticConcurrency` (OCC) behavior for nested paths in inclusion and exclusion lists.

#### Issues Fixed

* Excluding a parent path did not exclude its children
* Including a parent path did not include its nested fields
* Parent updates didn’t trigger OCC when child paths were included
* Ancestor paths incorrectly triggered OCC due to Mongoose’s `modifiedPaths` behavior

#### Solution

* Added leaf-level filtering to evaluate only the most specific modified paths
* Improved parent-child matching using `parentPaths`
* Ensured correct propagation of inclusion/exclusion rules
* Removed redundant OCC triggers from ancestor paths

#### Tests

* Added `test/gh-16054.test.js` (5 new test cases)
* All tests passing: 51 total (46 existing + 5 new)

#### Branch

`fix/gh-16054-nested-paths-occ`

prs.title: Login signup page
Fix Prisma schema relations and add migration
Fix prisma schema
Add MemberApprovalTable component and ClubLeaderDashboard page
Fix cursor type definition in ExternalMouseEvents
prs.body:
This PR adds proper TypeScript support for the cursor prop in chart components.
Previously, missing type definitions caused errors when passing a cursor value to components like BarChart.
Added a CursorProps interface, allowing booleans, React elements, CSS cursor values, or custom stroke options,
Fixes the long-standing type error reported by multiple users.

The cursor prop works at runtime, but TypeScript users faced type errors.
This ensures developers can pass cursor styles without hacks.

Testing: Verified the project builds successfully and tested with different valid cursor values.
No other chart components were affected.
